extraction.one_line_summary: KOOIJ Olav won chaotic sprint at 21yo after SWIFT Ben's 700m attack failed, while GOUDOU David clawed back 4 seconds on POGACAR at intermediate sprint.
tactical_patterns: GOUDU attacking intermediate sprints to claw back seconds when traditional sprint finishes aren't selective enough for time gaps.
SWIFT attacking at 700m on technical finish shows experienced domestiques exploiting chaos better than pure sprinters in dangerous finales.
Headwind all day successfully suppressed breakaway appetite despite 212km distance, both early attackers caught with 80km remaining.

rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: positive: At 21, beat top-tier sprinters through positioning in chaos rather than pure power, rebounding immediately from previous day's disappointment.
rider_signals.GOUDU David: positive: Proactively hunting 4-second intermediate sprint bonuses shows GC ambition beyond sprinting, now only 6 seconds behind yellow.
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: First failed sprint attempt of the week suggests declining ability to navigate technical/dangerous finishes.
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: neutral: 700m attack nearly succeeded, revealing veteran's tactical awareness exploits roundabout chaos better than pure sprint trains.
